programme.desc: In the remote Alaskan town of Bethel, a special task force is put together to track down a drug dealer; troopers are dispatched to an ATV accident near Sitka Sound.

programme.title: Alaska State Troopers

programme.title: Swamp Patrol
programme.desc: In Florida, troopers risk life and limb wading through gator-infested swamps to track a suspect; a thief in Georgia is close to becoming gator feed to evade capture; a man acting erratically makes a surprising getaway and a gator comes between a woman and her pizza.

programme.title: WWE Rivals
programme.desc: The Rock helped send WWE to new heights in the late 90s during the Attitude Era. But once Hollywood came calling, a bold and brash upstart named John Cena soon won over the entire WWE Universe and became the face of the company. However, Cena's public criticisms of The Rock led to verbal attacks both on and off the screen and eventually a dream match for WWE fans. Freddie Prinze Jr. breaks down th

programme.title: WWE Rivals
programme.desc: Former WWE writer Freddie Prinze Jr. leads a roundtable of legends and superstars that includes Kevin Nash, Kofi Kingston, Tamina and John Bradshaw Layfield, dissecting one of WWE's biggest on-screen rivalries in history.

programme.title: WWE Rivals
programme.desc: The on-screen rivalry between Kurt Angle and Brock Lesnar is a clash of two of the most highly decorated amateur wrestlers to ever lace up boots in WWE; personal issues outside the ring complicated things inside the squared circle.

programme.title: WWE Rivals
programme.desc: Spanning seven years, Brock Lesnar and Roman Reigns had one of the longest rivalries in WWE; when Brock made his return in 2012, Roman burst onto the scene and quickly rose to stardom.

programme.title: UFC Rivals
programme.desc: In the early 2000s, the two biggest names in mixed martial arts ignited a rivalry that would forever change the sport; Chuck Liddell and Tito Ortiz were once friends and training partners - but when competition and pride collided, their relationship exploded into one of the most legendary feuds in UFC history; Mario Lopez, CM Punk, Michael Bisping, Miesha Tate and Ken Shamrock break down the drama

programme.title: UFC Rivals
programme.desc: While Canadian superstar Georges St-Pierre looked unstoppable atop the Ultimate Fighting Championship in 2007, the odds were stacked against Matt Serra, who was widely seen as a fighter on the decline; but when a bold reality competition show matched the underdog against the dominant champion, it produced one of the greatest upsets in UFC history - sparking a rivalry no one saw coming; Mario Lopez

programme.title: WWE LFG
programme.desc: Elijah Holyfield continues his relationship with Apollo Crews, as they join forces in a tag team main event against Jaime Garcia and Malik Blade; Harlem Lewis looks to break from the pack while Braxton Cole receives a harsh dose of reality from Coach Bloom; Zena takes on the spicy Sirena Linton.

programme.title: WWE's Greatest Moments
programme.desc: The elimination chamber has struck fear into WWE superstars for over 20 years, leading to the creation of its own namesake event; relive some of the shocking moments from the premium live event, like Goldberg spearing Chris Jericho through the plexiglass in 2003, the first-ever women's elimination chamber in 2018, the debut of Kevin Owens as he takes on John Cena in 2015 and more.

programme.title: WWE's Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Making a WWE debut is one of the most nerve-wracking moments in a superstar's career. But for the fans, it's one of the most exciting things they can witness; the 2000s certainly proved to be a time of future Hall of Famers first appearing on WWE Television screens; we look back at the decade's most memorable WWE debuts, including Brock Lesnar, CM Punk, Booker T, Rey Mysterio, John Cena and more.

programme.desc: Ring-necked parakeets are some of Britain's most divisive birds. Are they beautiful, charismatic newcomers, or an ecological menace to crops and native wildlife? Chris Packham investigates.

programme.title: Dolly: The World's Most Famous Sheep
programme.desc: In 1996, a ragtag team of Scottish scientists made Dolly, the first mammal cloned from an adult cell, and changed history. From global fame to moral uproar, this is Dolly's story.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Norfolk, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet upcycler and bike enthusiast Simon. Their finds include a filing cabinet, a trunk and a classic bicycle.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer gets his thumb sliced off while trying to steal a brownie. Lisa misses the school bus. Bart discovers Fat Tony's stash of smuggled fireworks.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Ned opens a Christian amusement park. At first, the tame religious attractions are unpopular. But then visitors start having visions..

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Charlene White in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Indoor Fun with Sammy and Robby: Robin takes time off work to give Rebecca a romantic dream day out, but he's distracted by the chance to compete with Sam in a games marathon.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Carla agrees to split her late husband's $100 estate with his 'other widow' Gloria, but then she learns that he may have left her more than she thought.

programme.title: Cruising the Canary Islands with Susan Calman
programme.desc: Susan sets sail on a cruise to the sunny Canary Islands aboard the Regal Princess. First stop is the island of Gran Canaria.

programme.title: Inside The Tower of London
programme.desc: The Tower is back with its busiest year yet, marking D-Day with a huge celebration, revealing why Sir Walter Raleigh was locked up and saying goodbye to its much-loved Deputy Governor.

programme.title: Inside The Tower of London
programme.desc: The Tower prepares an ambitious display to celebrate the 80th anniversary of VE day, with almost 30,000 ceramic poppies cascading from the top of the White Tower. It's no small feat.

programme.title: 7 Wonders of the World with Bettany Hughes
programme.desc: On a breathtaking journey along Turkey's west coast, Bettany reimagines the Mausoleum of Halicarnassus, the Hanging Gardens of Babylon and Temple of Artemis.

programme.title: Heatwave: Summer of '76
programme.desc: The extraordinary story of a massive national moment when gardens burned, communities depended upon communal standpipes for their water and Brits were urged to "bath with a friend".

programme.title: The 1970s Kitchen: Everything We Loved & Lost
programme.desc: In this nostalgic show we chart the transformation of the British kitchen, revealing the trends and social changes that shaped the look and of our kitchens in the 70s.

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: National and international news.

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall tackles a retriever with bad breath, thanks to its preferred all-you-can-eat buffet from the dog poo bin. And Coco goes loco over inanimate objects when out for walks.

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Charity worker Julie and her two daughters swap homes and lives with millionaire consultant Jazz and fiancé Catia for a week. The result is a life-changing experience on both sides.

programme.title: Little Disasters
programme.desc: The threat of social services splitting up the family closes in, impacting the group. With her worst fears seemingly about to come true, Jess takes shocking action to keep Betsey safe.

programme.title: The Trial of Michael Jackson
programme.desc: The full story of the 2005 trial that took place in California, gripping the media, fans and public for four months, after 'The King of Pop' was arrested and charged with molestation.

programme.title: Freddie Mercury: A Secret Daughter
programme.desc: This landmark documentary lifts the lid on revelations that Freddie Mercury fathered a daughter in the 1970s, known as 'B'. Her death from cancer aged 48 was announced this week.
